description: Metadata ↔ data reconcile & index rebuild. Strata splits the S3-key→chunk map (meta: TiKV/Cassandra) from the chunk bytes (data: RADOS) — the scale advantage, but it drops the safety net RGW gets by colocating its index with the data. Today a RADOS chunk is an opaque random-OID blob with NO back-reference to its owning object, so the map is one-directional (meta→data): a restore skew silently leaks orphan chunks (GC walks meta→data, can't see them) or leaves dangling manifests (GET→5xx), and a lost meta backup means the intact bytes are permanently unreadable. This cycle makes every chunk self-describing (a back-reference xattr to its owner), ships a reconcile worker that repairs RADOS↔meta divergence after a restore (orphan chunks, dangling manifests), and a last-resort `strata admin rebuild-index` that reconstructs the manifest index from a data-tier scan. TiKV + Cassandra parity throughout. RESOLVED DECISIONS (post critical-review vs live code): (1) orphan default policy = report (never auto-delete; gc/restore explicit per-run); (2) back-reference payload = {schema_byte, bucket_id, key, version_id, chunk_idx, mtime} — mtime REQUIRED because version_id orders the chain but can't derive IsLatest when a suspended-null version coexists (the exact ListObjectVersions bug fixed in ralph/architecture-hardening); (3) rebuild-index is PLAINTEXT-ONLY — the wrapped DEK lives only in meta.Object.SSEKey, so a lost meta makes SSE ciphertext undecryptable; SSE objects are flagged unrecoverable, the back-reference carries NO key material (preserves the meta/data security separation); (4) S3-passthrough backend gets a back-reference too (object tags/metadata; reconciles via its native ListObjects, no pool-enumeration dependency); (5) reconcile = leader-elected worker draining admin-queued jobs (reuse the reshard US-005 pattern), rebuild = strata admin CLI one-shot (single-binary invariant). KNOWN INTERACTIONS: content-addressed dedup (ROADMAP P2) makes 1 chunk → N owners and is incompatible with a single-owner back-reference — the schema_byte reserves room for a future refcount-aware shape (CopyObject today re-writes chunks to fresh OIDs, so no multi-owner chunk exists yet). US-009 per-chunk CRC32C already SHIPPED in ralph/architecture-hardening (ChunkRef carries Checksum) — the CRC proves bytes are intact, this cycle's back-reference proves whose chunk it is. CI on Linux is the source of truth; Cassandra/RADOS failure modes do not reproduce on a local macOS/lima box. If a story cannot land in one Ralph iteration, split it into US-00N + a trailing story and record the split in progress.txt. Source PRD at tasks/prd-metadata-data-reconcile.md.

userStories.title: RADOS pool object-enumeration primitive (foundation)
userStories.description: As the reconcile/rebuild tooling, I need to enumerate every object in a RADOS pool, because orphan detection (a chunk the meta tier does not know about) and last-resort rebuild are ONLY possible by walking the pool itself — and there is no such primitive today (cephimpl exposes only GetPoolStats; the rebalance worker walks META via ListBucketsShard/ListObjects, never the pool).
acceptanceCriteria: cephimpl wraps go-ceph ioctx.Iter() (rados_nobjects_list) behind a small interface exposed on the main-module internal/data/rados shape: streams object IDs in a pool, resumable from a cursor/watermark, context-cancellable
Rate-limited via a token bucket (reuse the rebalance-style knob) so a live-cluster pool walk does not saturate OSDs
Filters to Strata chunk OIDs (skips foreign objects) and is namespace-aware for multi-cluster / per-class pools

Surfaced through the always-on rados package as a data.ErrRADOSNotCompiled-style sentinel off the ceph tag (mirrors rados.New), so the main module builds without librados (GOWORK=off go mod graph | grep -c go-ceph stays 0)
Red/green proof: seed N chunks into a test pool, enumerate -> exactly N OIDs; resume from a mid-walk cursor -> no dup, no drop
make vet passes; RADOS leg under ceph/integration (make test-rados); main-module build stays go-ceph-free
Tests pass

userStories.title: Chunk back-reference stamped at PUT (RADOS + S3 backend)
userStories.description: As the system, I want every chunk written to the data tier to carry a durable back-reference to its owning object so the data tier is self-describing and a reconcile/rebuild can run from data alone.
acceptanceCriteria: RADOS PutChunks stamps an xattr (user.strata.backref) carrying {schema_byte, bucket_id, key, version_id, chunk_idx, mtime} on each chunk, written in the SAME WriteOp as the chunk body via the existing writeChunkBatched SetXattr seam (one Operate, no extra round-trip)
mtime is REQUIRED (not optional): version_id orders the version chain but cannot derive IsLatest when a suspended-null version (ts=0) coexists — rebuild needs mtime to set IsLatest correctly

Payload is a compact versioned encoding; the leading schema_byte reserves room for a future refcount-aware / multi-owner shape (content-addressed dedup interaction); documented in code; carries NO key material (safe under SSE-S3/KMS — no plaintext, no wrapped DEK)
S3-passthrough backend leg: the S3 backend stamps the same back-reference as object tags / user-metadata on the backing object
Multipart and multi-cluster chunks covered (rides the existing per-chunk ioctx resolution)
Hot-path cost measured: a benchmark shows SetXattr-in-WriteOp adds negligible p99 vs bare WriteFull; numbers recorded in a bench doc
On by default with env opt-out STRATA_CHUNK_BACKREF (default on); off -> legacy no-xattr behaviour, reconcile/rebuild degrade gracefully and log that back-references are absent
make vet passes; memory backend no-ops; RADOS leg under ceph/integration; S3-backend leg under the s3 backend test seam
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Core back-reference shipped: data.Backref payload + EncodeBackref/DecodeBackref (versioned, no key material, mtime required) in internal/data/backref.go; ctx plumbing data.WithBackref/BackrefFromContext; STRATA_CHUNK_BACKREF env opt-out (default on, data.BackrefEnabledFromEnv); meta.NewVersionID() v1 minter; handler (putObject + copyObject) pre-decides version_id+mtime and pre-sets on ctx AND meta.Object so tiers agree; RADOS PutChunks stamps user.strata.backref in same WriteOp (cephimpl); S3-passthrough stamps x-amz-meta-strata-backref; memory no-ops. Multipart-origin chunks SPLIT to trailing US-001b (final version_id unknown at UploadPart). Bench negligible (38ns encode; one SetXattr rides existing WriteOp). cephimpl xattr read-back proof is CI-only (integration tag).

userStories.title: Chunk back-reference for multipart-origin chunks (trailing split)
userStories.description: As the system, I want multipart part chunks to carry the same back-reference as single-PUT chunks so a reconcile/rebuild can recover multipart objects from data alone. SPLIT from US-001 because part chunks are written at UploadPart/UploadPartCopy time, before the final object version_id is minted (at CompleteMultipartUpload) — so they currently land with NO back-reference (graceful degrade). See ROADMAP 'Known latent bugs'.
acceptanceCriteria: Multipart part chunks (UploadPart + UploadPartCopy) carry a back-reference recoverable to the final object {bucket_id, key, version_id, chunk_idx, mtime} after CompleteMultipartUpload

Approach decided + documented: either (a) stamp parts with the upload identity (uploadID) and rewrite version_id at Complete, or (b) a Complete-time re-stamp pass — whichever keeps the hot-path cost bounded; record the round-trip trade-off
version_id in the recovered back-reference matches the meta row the Complete writes (reconcile US-002 can pair the chunk with its manifest)
Red/green proof: multipart-upload an object, run rebuild-index against a wiped manifest -> object recovered with correct bytes + version order; matches the single-PUT path
make vet passes; RADOS leg under ceph/integration; S3-backend leg under the s3 backend test seam
ROADMAP 'Known latent bugs' entry closed in the same commit
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Trailing split from US-001. Lower urgency than the reconcile/rebuild line (US-002..US-004 function on single-PUT objects); pick up after the core cycle.

userStories.title: Reconcile worker — orphan chunk detection + policy
userStories.description: As an operator who just restored from backup, I want a pass that finds data-tier chunks with no matching manifest and resolves them by an explicit policy, so a restore skew does not leak storage forever.
acceptanceCriteria: A reconcile pass enumerates data-tier chunks (RADOS via the US-000 primitive per cluster/pool; S3 backend via its native ListObjects), reads each back-reference, and looks up the manifest in the meta store (TiKV + Cassandra parity)

Orphan chunk (chunk present, no manifest references it) resolved by a per-run policy: report (DEFAULT — never auto-delete), gc (enqueue for deletion — object was rolled back), or restore (rebuild the manifest row from the back-reference, for meta-older-than-data)

Execution model: leader-elected worker that DRAINS admin-queued jobs (reuse the reshard worker + admin-queue pattern — POST queues a job -> 202, worker runs out-of-band, GET reads progress); registered in registeredWorkerNames; per-iteration metrics.ObserveWorkerTick + StartIteration/EndIteration
Idempotent and resumable via a per cluster/pool watermark; a re-run after a partial pass converges
Rate-limited (token bucket, reuse the rebalance knob) so a reconcile on a live cluster does not saturate OSDs
Metrics: strata_reconcile_chunks_scanned_total, strata_reconcile_orphans_found_total{resolution}, strata_reconcile_errors_total

Red/green proof: seed a bucket, simulate restore skew (delete manifest rows, keep chunks); reconcile in gc mode -> chunks enqueued for deletion; restore mode -> manifest rebuilt and the object is GET-able again; report mode -> nothing deleted
make vet passes; memory + TiKV storetest contract; RADOS under integration
Tests pass

userStories.title: Reconcile worker — dangling manifest detection
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the pass to find manifests that point at chunks the data tier no longer has, so I learn which objects a restore left broken instead of discovering it on a client 5xx.
acceptanceCriteria: The reconcile pass walks manifests (meta -> data direction) and probes that each referenced chunk exists in the data tier
Dangling manifest (manifest present, chunk missing) is reported and, by policy, either quarantined (marked unreadable with a clear error) or deleted — NEVER silently left to surface as a corrupt GET
A summary report (per bucket: N orphans, N dangling, N healthy) is written to a report object / log, suitable for an operator go/no-go after restore
Metric strata_reconcile_dangling_manifests_total{resolution}
Red/green proof: seed objects, delete a chunk under one manifest, run reconcile -> that object reported dangling and quarantined; a healthy object untouched
make vet passes; memory + TiKV storetest contract; RADOS under integration
Tests pass

userStories.title: strata admin rebuild-index — last-resort rebuild from data scan (plaintext-only)
userStories.description: As an operator who lost the metadata backup, I want to reconstruct the manifest index for a bucket from the surviving data-tier chunks so the intact bytes are not permanently lost.
acceptanceCriteria: New strata admin rebuild-index subcommand (single-binary invariant — NOT a new binary, under cmd/strata/admin) that scans a cluster/pool (US-000 primitive), groups chunks by back-reference {bucket_id, key, version_id}, orders by chunk_idx, and writes reconstructed manifest rows into the meta store (TiKV + Cassandra)
Handles versioned objects (every version reconstructed; IsLatest correct across a suspended-null version using the back-reference mtime) and multipart-origin chunks
Idempotent + resumable; a re-run does not duplicate or corrupt rows already rebuilt
Refuses to overwrite a manifest that already exists in meta unless --force (live meta wins by default; rebuild is for the empty/lost case)
Gaps (missing chunk_idx in the sequence) reported, not silently stitched into a short object — a partial object is flagged, not served as whole

PLAINTEXT-ONLY: SSE-S3/KMS objects reported unrecoverable (wrapped DEK was in the lost meta) and NEVER silently served; object metadata absent from the back-reference (Content-Type, user-metadata, tags, ACL, storage-class, multipart-composite ETag) reported as lost, not fabricated; single-part ETag recomputed from rebuilt bytes

Red/green proof: seed a NON-SSE bucket, wipe its manifest rows entirely, run rebuild-index -> every object GET-able again with correct bytes + size + version order (IsLatest correct across a suspended-null version); a removed middle chunk -> object flagged gapped, not truncated; an SSE object -> reported unrecoverable, not served
make vet passes; TiKV + Cassandra meta targets; RADOS integration
Tests pass

userStories.title: Shared-responsibility ops note + reconcile runbook
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a clear statement of who owns what and how to run reconcile/rebuild after a restore, so I can make a safe go/no-go.
acceptanceCriteria: An operator doc states the boundary: operator owns TiKV/Cassandra and RADOS backup/restore; Strata owns the back-reference + reconcile/rebuild that realigns the two tiers after a restore (Strata does NOT reimplement br / nodetool)

A runbook section: post-restore procedure (run reconcile in report mode -> review summary -> choose gc/restore policy -> run rebuild-index only if the meta backup is unusable), with exact commands and what each metric means

States the rebuild-index recovery boundary LOUDLY: plaintext objects recover fully; SSE-S3/KMS objects are unrecoverable from data alone (wrapped DEK was in the lost meta); object metadata not in the back-reference is lost; the meta backup is the primary recovery path, rebuild-index is the last resort for plaintext bytes only
Cross-links the design prior art (Lustre LFSCK / HDFS fsck) so operators recognise the pattern
Docs build clean (make docs-build)

userStories.title: Reconcile / rebuild surfaced in the web console
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to trigger and watch a reconcile from the console (mirroring the drain/reshard-progress UX) rather than only via CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: An admin page / panel triggers a reconcile run (policy picker: report / gc / restore) and shows progress (scanned / orphans / dangling / % complete) reusing the DrainProgressBar / reshard-progress component pattern
The post-run summary report is viewable in the console

rebuild-index is intentionally CLI-ONLY (a destructive last-resort op gated behind shell access) — the console links to the runbook instead of exposing a one-click rebuild; documented as a deliberate choice
Playwright spec covers trigger -> progress -> summary
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: End-to-end reconcile/rebuild validation walkthrough
userStories.description: As the team, I want a single end-to-end exercise that proves the whole feature works together against the real backends, so the cycle is validated server-to-UI before close.
acceptanceCriteria: A documented walkthrough (script or runbook) seeds a bucket, simulates BOTH skew directions (meta-older-than-data and data-older-than-meta), runs reconcile report -> gc/restore, and verifies no silent leak and no silent corrupt GET — neither possible before this cycle
Wipes a non-SSE bucket's manifest rows and runs rebuild-index -> full recovery with correct bytes/size/version order, gaps flagged, SSE objects reported unrecoverable
Exercises the web console trigger -> progress -> summary path end-to-end (the UI is not a no-op stub)
Runs against both TiKV and Cassandra meta backends to prove parity
make vet + smoke pass; RADOS legs under integration; result recorded in progress.txt
Tests pass
